spec_match.rationale: Demonstrates the headline capability the spec promises: detect platformversion via high-entropy ua-ch. Aligned with the chromestatus motivation.

interactive.rationale: Live interactive controls present and wired up — passes the no-static-card rule.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb on the feature index closely matches what the concept page delivers — title, lede, and interactive surface line up.

edge_cases.rationale: Happy path is solid and several failure modes are probed, but the page misses a couple of the edge cases the spec calls out (see open questions).

references.rationale: No see-also section and no chromestatus.com/feature link on this concept page; relies on the parent feature index for the canonical link. The self-heal script needs it embedded on every page.

design_system.rationale: Uses CSS variables throughout, no raw hex creeping in, button colours and contrast appear within AA.

openQuestions.title: Show what each cut historically broke (which APIs landed in macOS 13/14/15)
openQuestions.detail: The cut-off table is purely calendar. Pair each row with the visible developer-facing impact (e.g. macOS 13 unlocked iOS-style Lockdown Mode, font fallback changes) so authors understand the cost of staying on an older OS, not just whether Chrome will keep updating.

openQuestions.title: Embed the chromestatus.com/feature/<id> link in this concept page
openQuestions.detail: The self-heal recovery script relies on every concept page carrying the canonical chromestatus link in a see-also or references block. Add it: https://chromestatus.com/feature/5077742779498496

summary: Live UA-CH probe + free-form version input + full historical cut-off table. Main gaps: Show what each cut historically broke (which APIs landed in macOS 13/14/15); Embed the chromestatus.com/feature/<id> link in this concept page.
